The order Malpighiales is a group of flowering plants that also includes plants such as the mulberries and the cunjea. intro: Erythrococca mildbraedii, also known as the African bitter leaf, is a plant species in the family Euphorbiaceae, native to tropical Africa. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include treating fever, diarrhea, and other gastrointestinal issues, as well as its potential in reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular health. The bioactive constituents of Erythrococca mildbraedii include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects. In traditional African medicine, the plant is often prepared as a decoction, infusion, or tincture, which can be used to treat various health conditions, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
